__comment: HAND-OFF STUB — NOT A REAL REALM EXPORT. Replace the contents of this file with the output of `kcadm.sh get realms/qyl` once the realm has been provisioned. This placeholder exists so that ops can see the required settings in one place without having to reconstruct them from documentation.
__TODO_ops_checklist: 1. Provision Keycloak 25+ instance (loopback-port wildcards are rejected by <25).
3. Create public client with the settings below.
4. Add the audience mapper so end-user tokens carry the qyl-mcp-user audience.
5. Enable Dynamic Client Registration on the realm (Realm Settings → Client Registration → Anonymous Access Policies → 'trusted-hosts' allow).
6. Run `kcadm.sh get realms/qyl > infra/keycloak/qyl-realm.json` and replace this stub entirely.
7. Commit the real export. Treat this file as infrastructure-as-code — diffs must be reviewed.

__required_client_settings.name: qyl MCP (public, PKCE only)
__required_client_settings.description: Public client used by Claude Code / Claude Desktop / any MCP-capable end-user agent to authenticate against mcp.qyl.info via authorization_code + PKCE.

__required_audience_mapper.name: qyl-mcp-audience
__required_audience_mapper.__note: This adds 'qyl-mcp-user' to the `aud` claim of every access token issued to this client. The qyl.mcp server is configured with QYL_KEYCLOAK_AUDIENCE=qyl-mcp-user so tokens without this audience fail JwtBearer validation with a 401.

__required_realm_settings.__note_dcr: Dynamic Client Registration (DCR) is a separate setting at the realm level under Realm Settings → Client Registration. Enable the 'openid-connect' registration endpoint at /realms/qyl/clients-registrations/openid-connect. Without DCR, every user would need manual pre-registration — see docs/mcp-oauth-runbook.md → 'Failure mode: DCR disabled'.
__required_realm_settings.__note_https: 'external' requires HTTPS for requests outside the internal network. For local dev against http://localhost:8080, set to 'none' temporarily — never in production.
